title: Not (yet) the whole story: Evaluating Visual Storytelling Requires More than Measuring Coherence, Grounding, and Repetition

methodology.approach: Novel evaluation method for visual storytelling
methodology.evaluation_type: Reference-free evaluation that quantifies distance between human-written and model-generated stories
methodology.key_feature: Assesses story quality independently from overlap with ground-truth stories

models_evaluated.name: TAPM
models_evaluated.finding: Performs slightly worse than LLaVA but close

models_evaluated.name: Upgraded TAPM
models_evaluated.characteristics: Upgraded with better visual and language components
models_evaluated.finding: Achieves comparable performance to LLaVA with significantly fewer parameters

key_findings: LLaVA performs best on visual storytelling task but only slightly better than TAPM
TAPM is 50 times smaller than LLaVA
Upgraded TAPM achieves comparable performance to LLaVA with significantly fewer parameters
Stories generated by LLaVA and upgraded TAPM are quantitatively close to human stories
Human evaluations show humans still prefer human-written stories despite quantitative closeness
contributions: Proposed a novel evaluation method for visual storytelling quantifying distance between human-written and model-generated stories
Used the evaluation to assess various models, finding LLaVA achieves the best result
Leveraged insights to upgrade TAPM, achieving performance comparable to LLaVA
